title: Chicken Shawarma
tagline: Street-style spiced chicken wraps with a quick garlic-mayo toum, layered to shop-level.
servingsNote: 2 loaded wraps; scale the marinade for weekly meal prep
macroNotes: Solid protein (~50 g/serving), but fat runs high from thighs + the mayo garlic sauce. Fine on a normal training day, heavy on a cut.
To cut ~150–200 kcal per serving: use chicken breast instead of thigh (slice thin, don't overcook), and make the toum with more yogurt and less mayo (e.g. 2 tbsp mayo + 3 tbsp Greek yogurt).
Add pickles and cabbage/onion freely — near-zero calories and they cut the richness.

items: 500 g boneless chicken thighs (juicier) — or breast for a leaner wrap
1 tsp paprika or Kashmiri chilli powder (colour, not heat)
1/2 tsp garlic powder (or 4–5 crushed garlic cloves)
1/2 tsp cinnamon, allspice or garam masala
Optional: 1 tbsp tahini (very authentic Middle Eastern note)

ingredients.group: Basin-toss finish (do not skip — this is the shop trick)

items: Optional: fries, for a Lebanese-style wrap

steps.title: Marinate
steps.body: Mix the marinade — 3 tbsp curd, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1/2–1 tsp pepper, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp ginger paste, 1/2 tsp cinnamon/garam masala, 1 tsp salt (and 1 tbsp tahini if using) — and coat 500 g chicken thighs. Rest at least 1 hour, ideally overnight. For the juiciest street-style bite, slice thin.

steps.title: Make the garlic sauce
steps.body: Stir 4 tbsp mayo, 1 tbsp yogurt, 2–3 grated garlic cloves, 1/2–1 tsp lemon juice, salt and pepper (and 1 tsp tahini if using) until smooth. This is what makes shawarma taste like shawarma. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook the chicken
steps.body: STOVE-TOP (best browning): sear on a hot cast-iron/heavy pan with a drizzle of oil, medium-high, until char spots appear, flip, cook through. OTG: 220°C, roast thin slices 12–14 minutes, then grill/broil 2–3 minutes for char — flip at the 10–12 min mark and don't leave thin slices a full 20 minutes or they dry out. AIR FRYER: 190°C for 12–15 minutes, shake once, then 200°C for 2–3 minutes to brown.

steps.title: Rest & slice
steps.body: Rest the chicken 2–3 minutes so the juices settle, then slice into thin shawarma strips.

steps.title: Basin-toss (the key upgrade)
steps.body: Put the hot chicken in a bowl and toss with 1 tsp butter, 1 tsp lemon juice and 1 tbsp of the garlic sauce. This is exactly what shawarma shops do before wrapping — it makes the meat softer, juicier and saucier, and rescues breast if you used it.

steps.title: Assemble in layers
steps.body: Warm the pita/rumali. Spread a base layer of garlic sauce, lay the chicken in a long even strip (not one central mound), add pickles/jalapeños, then a second thin drizzle of garlic sauce through the middle, a little cabbage/onion for crunch, and an optional streak of hot sauce. Layering the sauce twice — not just against the bread — is what pushes it to 10/10.

steps.title: Roll & toast
steps.body: Roll tight. For the shop-style crust, brush a tiny bit of oil or butter on the outside and toast the wrapped roll on a tawa until golden with light char spots.

notes: Thighs are more forgiving and stay juicy through the longer home cook; breast works but slice it thin and pull it early, then rely on the basin-toss to keep it soft.
The biggest quality jump comes from even chicken distribution, double-layering the sauce, and the butter/lemon basin-toss.
A little cabbage or lightly-salted onion adds crunch, moisture and structure without changing the flavour.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
